meaning: n.鐩茬洰鐨勭埍鍥藉績,娌欐枃涓讳箟
examples: It is also called male and female chauvinism.
Betty had no idea what male chauvinism was.
On this point, oddly, Europe's nationalists shed their chauvinism for a saccharine solidarity beyond borders.
Their chauvinism meant they assumed that the conspirators they were seeking must be men.
The hope of forging a common European identity has given way to greater national assertiveness, even chauvinism.
